InsurTech Intelligence

Briefing Diario

Viernes 29 de mayo de 2026  ·  805 palabras  ·  4 min de lectura

Los supervisores marcan la agenda hoy: Exclusivo centra el debate regulatorio con implicaciones para todo el mercado asegurador. La jornada se completa con Palomar, que pone el foco en otro frente del mercado: el de inversión. De los 134 artículos rastreados hoy, 50 han activado nuestras alertas de señal — una densidad de noticias relevante que no veíamos en días. A continuación, las cinco historias que hemos seleccionado entre 134 artículos de más de 40 fuentes, ordenadas por impacto real sobre el negocio asegurador.

1. ⚖️ Regulación

[Exclusivo] Bankaool de México a poco de cerrar adquisición de banco en Brasil

La tradicional ruta de expansión de Brasil a México ahora tiene viaje de regreso, debido a que el grupo costarricense Omni, accionista mayoritario de Bankaool de México, está a poco de concretar la compra de un banco en Brasil.   2. ¿Por qué importa? Puede requerir adaptación regulatoria en los próximos meses.
  3. ¿Qué vigilar? Las aseguradoras deberán revisar sus modelos de cumplimiento y posiblemente provisionar costes de adaptación normativa.

2. 💰 Inversión

El límite de reaseguro contra terremotos de Palomar asciende a 3.920 millones de dólares tras la renovación de junio

Palomar Holdings, Inc., una compañía de seguros especializada con sede en EE. UU. centrada en cobertura de propiedad y accidentes, completó con éxito su colocación de reaseguro el 1 de junio, asegurando aproximadamente $421 millones de límite incremental para respaldar el crecimiento de su franquicia de terremotos. Mac Armstrong, presidente y director ejecutivo de Palomar, dijo: “Estamos muy satisfechos con el resultado de nuestra reunión del 1 de junio.

  1. El dato clave: El límite de reaseguro contra terremotos de Palomar asciende a 3.920 millones de dólares tras la renovación en junio.
  2. La operación: $3920M, una de las mayores transacciones detectadas en el sector en las últimas semanas.
  3. ¿Qué vigilar? El capital invertido anticipa las áreas donde los incumbentes afrontarán mayor presión competitiva en los próximos 12-24 meses.

Leer en Reinsurance News →

3. 🤝 M&A

Athora completa la sucesión del CEO

Athora Holding, un grupo europeo de servicios de ahorro y jubilación, anunció la finalización de la sucesión prevista de su director ejecutivo, con Todd Solash asumiendo formalmente el papel de director ejecutivo del grupo. La transición de liderazgo se produce tras la adquisición de Pension Insurance Corporation por parte de Athora y una emisión de derechos de capital ordinario relacionada por valor de 3.500 millones de euros. Tras la transacción, Athora dijo que ahora gestiona 1 euro

  1. El dato clave: La transición de liderazgo se produce tras la adquisición de Pension Insurance Corporation por parte de Athora y una emisión de derechos de capital ordinario relacionada por valor de 3.500 millones de euros.
  2. ¿Por qué importa? Consolida el mercado y puede redefinir la competencia en el sector.
  3. ¿Qué vigilar? La consolidación reduce actores independientes y abre oportunidades para distribuidores y proveedores de tecnología.

4. 💰 Inversión

CEA apunta a un aumento de 400 millones de dólares en reaseguro contra terremotos del bono catalítico Sutter Re 2026-1

La Autoridad de Terremotos de California (CEA) está buscando aumentar su último patrocinio de bonos catastróficos, con el objetivo de recaudar un tercio a $400 millones de dólares de protección de reaseguro contra terremotos totalmente garantizado de esta Sutter Re Ltd. Para esta emisión de Sutter Re Serie 2026-1, la CEA inicialmente apuntaba a $300 millones de reaseguro contra terremotos totalmente garantizado de los mercados de capital. Las

  1. El dato clave: CEA apunta a un aumento de 400 millones de dólares en reaseguro contra terremotos del bono catalán Sutter Re 2026-1.
  2. ¿Por qué importa? Nueva financiación que puede acelerar la competencia en el sector.
  3. ¿Qué vigilar? El capital invertido anticipa las áreas donde los incumbentes afrontarán mayor presión competitiva en los próximos 12-24 meses.

5. 💰 Inversión

Heritage obtiene “ahorros sustanciales de costos” al renovar 2.200 millones de dólares en reaseguro y límite de bonos catástrofes

Heritage Insurance Holdings, Inc., una aseguradora de propiedad y accidentes con sede en Florida, de expansión nacional, ha renovado el límite de reaseguro y bonos de catástrofe por un total de $2.2 mil millones para 2026, y el director ejecutivo de la empresa citó “ahorros sustanciales de costos” y más protección plurianual. “Me gustaría agradecer a nuestros dedicados socios de reaseguros que han apoyado nuestro negocio a través de múltiples catástrofes.

  1. El dato clave: Heritage obtiene “ahorros sustanciales de costos” al renovar 2.200 millones de dólares en reaseguro y límite de bonos de catástrofe.
  2. La operación: $2200M, una de las mayores transacciones detectadas en el sector en las últimas semanas.
  3. ¿Qué vigilar? El capital invertido anticipa las áreas donde los incumbentes afrontarán mayor presión competitiva en los próximos 12-24 meses.

Jefferies Sued by Fund Investors Alleging Water Firm Fraud

has been hit with an investor class action lawsuit over bond purchases made by its 352 Capital fund in a water vending machine firm that federal prosecutors have called a scam. According to the complaint filed in New York state court, Leucadia Asset Management’s 352 Capital poured millions of dollars into bonds issued by Water Station Management, which the government and Jefferies itself have alleged was a fraud. In the latest lawsuit, the fund’s investors allege that Leucadia approved these bond purchases despite knowing the underlying collateral did not exist, and despite awareness that Chirico was personally invested in the water machine business.

Carrier Management · ahora mismo · 1 min Nuevo 🌪️ Clima & Catástrofes General

With a Super El Niño on the Horizon, Insurers Have Narrow a Window to Get Communications Right

With Super El Niño impacts expected to begin as early as the end of summer , the window for meaningful policyholder outreach is already open. Climate scientists are increasingly confident that a Super El Niño will emerge later this year—and that it will rank amongst the most powerful on record—impacting global temperature and rainfall patterns. One of the most important things insurers can do right now is make sure policyholders know exactly what their coverage includes—and excludes—before they need to use it.

💡Impacto directo en exposición, pricing y reservas del sector.

Navigating The DPI Crunch And Startup Funding

AI’s share of venture funding climbed to 80% this quarter, up from 55% a year ago. The exit window for American founders is narrower than the headline funding numbers suggest. Crunchbase reported that global venture deployment hit roughly $300 billion in Q1 2026, with $188 billion of that, about 65%, concentrated in four companies: OpenAI , Anthropic , xAI and Anduril Industries .

Artemis · ahora mismo · 1 min Nuevo 💰 Inversión General

CEA targets upsized $400m of earthquake reinsurance from Sutter Re 2026-1 cat bond

The California Earthquake Authority (CEA) is looking to upsize its latest catastrophe bond sponsorship, with the target raised one-third to $400 million of fully-collateralized earthquake reinsurance protection from this Sutter Re Ltd. For this Sutter Re Series 2026-1 issuance, the CEA was initially targeting $300 million of fully-collateralized earthquake reinsurance from the capital markets. Last year, the CEA successfully sponsored three series of catastrophe bonds, securing the company in total $1.57 billion of earthquake reinsurance protection from the cat bond market.

𝕏 in
Artemis · ahora mismo · 1 min Nuevo General

Despite softening, reinsurance pricing at 2021/2022 levels “widely viewed as adequate” – KBW

In general, analysts from KBW heard during a recent Bermuda trip that reinsurance pricing is down about 20% at the mid-year renewals (the report is headlined “20% is the new 15%”), but even after the softening taking rates back to levels seen in 2021 to 2022, this is still “widely viewed as adequate”, the analysis explains. Summing up on pricing that, “We heard several comparisons of this year’s pricing to 2021/2022 levels, which is still widely viewed as adequate and a far cry from the low point of 2017.” Terms and conditions are seen as “mostly holding”, albeit with some concessions that improve coverage for reinsurance buyers. KBW’s analysts said, “Most reinsurers appear far more willing to accept lower pricing than to yield changes on the much improved T&C changes that were achieved at the 1/1/2023 onset of the recent hardening cycle.” But, the analysts cite a “gamut of minor T&C changes”, such: as minimum premium true-ups to exposure now being allowed to float down and up; Florida reinsurance premiums now being paid quarterly instead of upfront; broadening of retrocession covers to named natural perils, from named specific perils (this is one to watch if events happen); aggregates, cascading covers, and top and drops now being more widely available; plus some modest extension of hours clauses and radius definitions (again, ones to watch when events occur).

Leer artículo →
𝕏 in
Reinsurance News · ahora mismo · 1 min Nuevo ⚖️ Regulación General

T&Cs hold firm, catastrophe reinsurance rates down closer to 20% at mid-year renewals: KBW

KBW, the investment bank and financial services research firm specialising in the insurance sector, has indicated a more challenging mid-year catastrophe reinsurance renewal environment for sellers, with pricing reductions nearer 20% than 15%, particularly for Florida-focused business, while terms and conditions (T&Cs) are seen as largely holding firm. On pricing, KBW estimated that headline risk-adjusted reductions for mid-year property catastrophe renewals are averaging around 20%. While property and property cat reinsurance rates continue to soften, KBW noted that the more significant development across the market is that the stricter terms and conditions introduced during the 2023 hardening cycle have largely remained intact, despite a series of smaller concessions beginning to emerge.

💡Puede requerir adaptación regulatoria en los próximos meses.

Reinsurance News · ahora mismo · 1 min Nuevo ⚖️ Regulación General

Vishal Desai appointed as International Chief Risk Officer at MS&AD Insurance Group

MS&AD Insurance Group, a major Japanese non-life insurance group with a substantial international presence, has appointed Vishal Desai to a senior global role as International Chief Risk Officer. In this capacity, Desai will join the International Executive Committee at MS&AD Insurance Group, working alongside experienced colleagues from across the organisation’s global network. He will collaborate with entity leadership teams to support the continued development of MS&AD Insurance Group as an international insurance and financial services group, with a focus on strengthening risk oversight, compliance and legal governance across the International portfolio.

Palomar’s earthquake reinsurance limit up to $3.92bn after June renewal

Palomar Holdings, Inc., a US-based specialty insurance company focused on property and casualty coverage, has successfully completed its June 1st reinsurance placement, securing approximately $421 million of incremental limit to support the growth of its earthquake franchise. Mac Armstrong, Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of Palomar, said, “We are very pleased with the outcome of our June 1 reinsurance placement and remain grateful for the support of our broad and diversified reinsurance panel. The company’s reinsurance coverage now extends to $3.92 billion for earthquake events and $135 million for continental US hurricane events.

Viewpoint: Somali Piracy Is Back – Fueled by Political Turmoil, Aid Cuts and Iran War

In our 2014 research on the local dynamics of Somali piracy, we found that peaks in local pirate activity coincided with periods of political turmoil and military contest. Together, these incidents have sparked concern over whether we’re seeing a resurgence of Somali piracy. Since then, Somali piracy has been kept in check by a combination of private security guards, naval patrols and land-based development initiatives.

Lemonade centralizes internal knowledge with Guru

Lemonade is using Guru as an internal knowledge management platform to help employees across customer service, claims, and underwriting access and maintain insurance-related information. INESE · hace 5h · 1 min Nuevo ⚖️ Regulación General

Mapfre lidera al seguro español en el ranking de marcas más valiosas

En conjunto, el ranking refleja una evolución desigual dentro del sector asegurador español, con Mapfre ganando posiciones mientras otras compañías registran movimientos más limitados. Mapfre refuerza su liderazgo como principal marca aseguradora en España y mejora su posición en el ranking nacional de Brand Finance . En 2025, las marcas más valiosas de España alcanzaron un valor conjunto de 132.200 millones de euros, impulsadas principalmente por el buen desempeño de la banca, las aerolíneas y el sector cervecero, que lideran el crecimiento del valor de marca en el país.

INESE · hace 5h · 1 min Nuevo 💰 Inversión General

Las grandes aseguradoras disparan su valor de marca un 14% y marcan su mejor resultado en cinco años

El sector asegurador mundial ha alcanzado su mejor desempeño en valor de marca en los últimos cinco años, según ha dado a conocer Bran Finance . En la cúspide del ranking se mantiene Ping An Insurance , que supera los 39.900 millones de dólares de valor de marca tras avanzar un 19% y encadena una década como líder mundial. El ranking refleja además un refuerzo del peso de Estados Unidos y China en el sector , que representan conjuntamente casi la mitad del valor total del ranking y marcan el ritmo de crecimiento, apoyados en la escala de sus mercados y en la mejora de la eficiencia operativa.

Assicuratrice Milanese anuncia su entrada en Caución y pone al frente a Julent Augusto Cerezo

Como dio a conocer ayer por su perfil de LinkedIn, Assicuratrice Milanese España sigue reforzando su presencia en el mercado español e inicia su desarrollo en el ramo de Caución. Como primer paso, h a incorporado a Julent Augusto Cerezo Cendales como responsable de Suscripción del ramo en España, reportando directamente a Marco Zamparo , director del Ramo de Cauciones, ambos en la foto. En este sentido, adelanta que pondrá el foco en: Caución Técnica frente a la Administración Pública; Puntos de Conexión; y Garantías de Afianzamiento de Cantidades Entregadas a Cuenta.

Trump Files Fresh $10 Billion Suit Over WSJ’s Epstein Story

over an article about his alleged close ties to Jeffrey Epstein, after an earlier version of the suit was tossed out by a judge. The revised suit was filed late Wednesday in federal court in Florida, meeting a deadline set by a judge who dismissed the original complaint for lacking sufficient claims that the story was filed with “actual malice” toward Trump, a high bar set for libel suits by public figures. Trump sued over a July 2025 story that said he’d sent a “bawdy” birthday note to the disgraced financier in 2003, typed within a sketched outline of a naked woman and signed with the president’s signature in the pubic area.

Claims Journal · hace 5h · 1 min Nuevo General

Court Overturns Insulet’s $59M Insulin-Pump Trade Secret Verdict

appeals court on Thursday overturned a $59 million verdict that medical device maker Insulet won against Korean rival EOFlow for allegedly stealing trade secrets related to its insulin-pump technology. A federal jury in Massachusetts had determined in 2024 that EOFlow owed $452 million in damages for misappropriating Insulet trade secrets to create a competitor to Insulet’s Omnipod, a wearable insulin pump for diabetes patients. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit reversed the verdict after finding that Insulet had waited too long to bring its lawsuit.

Artemis · hace 5h · 1 min Nuevo 💰 Inversión 💰 $2623M General

Universal lifts reinsurance tower to $2.623bn, ILS capital from Nephila a key participant

once again expanded its reinsurance limit at the 2026 mid-year renewal, lifting the top of the reinsurance tower to $2.623 billion, with institutional capital from ILS manager Nephila Capital a key participant in the renewal again for the insurer. But its partnership with Nephila Capital as a key reinsurance market has been in effect for over a decade now, as the ILS investment manager has been cited as one of the most significant renewal participants for Universal since at least 2014, which was when we first covered it . Universal continues to access reinsurance capital from all sources, but recall that the insurer has not sponsored a catastrophe bond since 2021, which sets it apart from many of its Florida domiciled, but also nationally expansive competitors these days.

💡Nueva financiación que puede acelerar la competencia en el sector.

𝕏 in
Artemis · hace 5h · 1 min Nuevo 🌪️ Clima & Catástrofes General

Gallagher teams with FloodFlash to launch parametric flood cover for golf courses

Insurance broker and risk adviser Gallagher has collaborated with FloodFlash, a NormanMax company, to launch a new parametric insurance solution designed to protect golf courses from mitigate financial disruption caused by flooding and severe weather. Intense rainfall as well as extreme weather events such as hurricanes and coastal surge events can cause flooding and pose significant challenges for golf courses, especially for venues located near rivers, lakes or coastal zones In light of this, the new parametric insurance solution being introduced by Gallagher and FloodFlash aims to offer a more rapid and adaptable means of financial protection against these risks. Gallagher’s specialist parametric team will reportedly work with clients in order to ensure that the product provides comprehensive cover complimenting any traditional insurance golf course owners may have in place and provide essential additional cover for natural catastrophes and weather events.

💡Impacto directo en exposición, pricing y reservas del sector.

Artemis · hace 5h · 1 min Nuevo 💰 Inversión 💰 $2200M General

Heritage gets “substantial cost savings” as it renews $2.2bn of reinsurance and cat bond limit

Heritage Insurance Holdings, Inc., a nationally expansive, Florida headquartered property and casualty insurer, has renewed reinsurance and catastrophe bond limit totalling $2.2 billion for 2026, with the firm’s CEO citing “substantial cost savings” and more multi-year protection. “I would like to thank our dedicated reinsurance partners who have supported our business through multiple catastrophic events over the last several years and look forward to their continued partnership as we work to prudently grow the Company.” With reinsurance and cat bond pricing softened, Heritage has made a substantial $63.2 million saving on its reinsurance renewal this year, although it is not clear how the lower total limit placed factors into things. We placed more multi-year coverage this year and achieved substantial reinsurance cost savings while providing enhanced vertical protection for each of our insurance entities.

Liberty Mutual said it plans to offer conversational AI quoting in more than 40 states by the end of the year. Newport Specialty Partners, a specialty insurance platform that works with managing general agents (MGAs) and programme administrators, has appointed Mary Sbaschnig as its new Chief Operating Officer and Chief Information Officer. Dennis DiCapua, Chief Executive Officer of Newport Specialty Partners, commented: “Mary brings exactly the combination of insurance operating experience, technology expertise, and practical MGA knowledge that we want at Newport.” DiCapua continued: “She understands how underwriting businesses actually operate, how technology can improve decision-making and efficiency, and how to build the infrastructure needed to support disciplined growth. Mary’s leadership will be instrumental in strengthening the capabilities that support our partner companies while preserving the entrepreneurial culture and underwriting discipline that define Newport.” Sbaschnig said Newport’s model of supporting specialist insurance businesses while allowing them to retain their individual strengths was a key factor in her decision to join the company.

Ohio to Halt Data Center Tax Credits as Opposition Grows

Ohio will pause incentives for future data center projects, following a directive from the governor to halt the tax credits while a committee studies the impact of the projects on the state’s economy. “As this work is ongoing, I believe it is appropriate for the Ohio Tax Credit Authority to pause its consideration of new data center tax exemptions while the full impact of data center growth in Ohio is being reviewed,” he added. About $64 billion of data centers projects have either been canceled or delayed because of community opposition, according to industry researcher Data Center Watch.

Insurance Journal · hace 5h · 1 min Nuevo 👔 Cambio de Liderazgo General

Pollution and Forever Chemicals Threaten Health of Michigan’s Rivers

“Just looking at settlement patterns in Michigan tells us a lot about how important our rivers are,” said Lisa Dechano-Cook, a Western Michigan University geographer who co-wrote the book ” Kalamazoo River .” “You wanted to be near water, especially running water, so that you could get from place to place.” When combined, Michigan’s 76,000 miles of rivers, creeks and streams have a shoreline 46 times the length of Michigan’s Great Lakes coast. Even some of the state’s most well-known inland lakes — from Oakland County’s Kent Lake to central Michigan’s Lake Ovid — are actually rivers that have been slowed, deepened and widened by dams. “I really don’t think (rivers)get the proper credit and thought,” said Melissa DeSimone, executive director of the Michigan Lakes and Streams Association.

Insurance Innovation Reporter · hace 18h · 1 min Nuevo 🌪️ Clima & Catástrofes General

EHAB Launches Parametric Weather Platform for Brokers

EHAB (London), a climate-risk technology company specializing in weather intelligence, operational climate risk and parametric insurance infrastructure, has launched WeatherWise Studio, a parametric weather structuring and workflow platform for brokers, insurers and reinsurers. EHAB says the platform combines more than 45 years of global weather history at approximately 9 km resolution with a library of industry-specific parametric structures for sectors including renewable energy, construction, logistics, agriculture, hospitality, commercial real estate and infrastructure. The platform, which has been in beta with selected broker partners since January 2026, is designed to let brokers move from an initial client discussion to a structured parametric submission in under 30 minutes, according to the company.

NHTSA Opens Probe into 115,000 Rivian Vehicles

National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said on Thursday it has opened a preliminary probe into 114,922 Rivian vehicles over an issue with the rear toe link. NHTSA’s probe will assess the sensitivity of the rear toe link joint to foreseeable road and service conditions and evaluate Rivian’s current toe link repair procedure, among other steps. The agency’s office of defects investigation received two vehicle owner questionnaires reporting that the left rear toe link separates while driving, causing vehicles to swerve across multiple lanes of traffic, NHTSA said.

Ninety-five percent of South Carolina respondents and 93 percent of North Carolina respondents identified distracted drivers as a primary contributor to work zone crashes and intrusions. Moody’s: U.S. Faces $375B in Uninsured Flood Losses From 1-in-100-Year Event

faces a widening gap between growing flood risk and the take-up of flood insurance, potentially exposing the nation to more than $375 billion in aggregated uninsured flood losses from a 1-in-100-year event, according to a new whitepaper from Moody’s. Counties in Florida, Louisiana, South Carolina and Texas face more than $5 billion in potential uninsured losses in the event of a 1-in-100-year flood. “Uninsured losses arise not from isolated outliers, but from persistent gaps between expanding flood hazards – particularly beyond regulatory flood maps that dictate mortgage requirements, as well as rarer, high-severity events – and insurance take-up,” Moody’s concluded.

Reinsurance News · hace 18h · 1 min Nuevo 🌪️ Clima & Catástrofes General

Federal insurance backstop to boost Canada’s resilience to earthquake risk: Morningstar DBRS

Morningstar DBRS, a credit rating agency focused on financial institutions and insurance, together with the Property and Casualty Insurance Compensation Corporation (PACICC), a Canadian industry-funded organisation that supports property and casualty policyholders, and the Insurance Bureau of Canada (IBC), the national industry association representing private property and casualty insurers, examine the ongoing policy discussion around introducing a federally supported insurance backstop for earthquake risk in Canada. The analysis also references the Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions (OSFI), Canada’s federal insurance regulator responsible for capital adequacy and prudential oversight, alongside international developments such as proposals from the European Insurance and Occupational Pensions Authority and the European Stability Mechanism aimed at strengthening catastrophe risk-sharing frameworks. Following this, PACICC and the Insurance Bureau of Canada have jointly proposed a structured backstop model inspired by the US Terrorism Risk Insurance Act (TRIA), under which the US government shares insured losses with private insurers once defined thresholds are reached.

appeals court on Thursday overturned a $59 million verdict that medical device maker Insulet won against Korean rival EOFlowfor allegedly stealing trade secrets related to its insulin-pump technology. Reinsurance buyers in Florida continue to turn to and leverage the catastrophe bond amidst rising appetite for risks from the state and softening of property catastrophe rates, with over $3.2 billion of new Florida-focused cat bonds having been issued thus far for 12 sponsors in 2026 year-to-date (up to May 12, 2026), according to Guy Carpenter. Reinsurance News · hace 23h · 1 min Nuevo General

Global P&C insurance market stabilises as aggressive rate hikes fade: Allianz Research

The global property and casualty (P&C) insurance market is moving from a pricing boom towards normalisation after a period of aggressive rate hikes, according to Allianz Research’s latest “Global Insurance Report.” The insurer’s data shows that global P&C premiums grew by +3.8% in 2025, well below both the prior year’s +8.5% surge and the segment’s 10 year compound average growth rate (CAGR) of +5.6%. Supported by aging demographics and high savings, Asia remains the largest global life insurance market, analysts noted. While North America remained the industry’s dominant market, accounting for 52% of global P&C premiums, its growth plummeted to +2.2% from +9.7% in the previous year.

Merchant Ships Desert Strait of Hormuz Amid Renewed US Strikes

Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz appeared all but deserted on Thursday as commercial operators remain wary of renewed military escalation .No commercial vessels were spotted transiting the waterway Thursday morning as tensions spiked following a second round of US strikes against Iranian military targets this week. Tehran also said 26 ships crossed the strait in the past day, which could include smaller, coastal vessels. President Donald Trump said no single nation would be allowed to control the Strait of Hormuz, underscoring one of the central obstacles to securing a lasting agreement with Iran.

Coverager · hace 23h · 1 min Nuevo General

Sircon Compensation recognized for helping carriers turn agent compensation into a competitive advantage

The post Sircon Compensation recognized for helping carriers turn agent compensation into a competitive advantage appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . With solutions to manage agent onboarding, licensing, compensation and compliance across personal, commercial, life and health lines of business, Sircon for Carriers is powering Distribution Velocity for insurers so they can take advantage of every opportunity, adapt faster and grow smarter. Built specifically for insurance, Sircon Compensation helps carriers design and manage complex commission and incentive programs, automate payment processing, and gain real-time visibility into producer compensation across distribution channels.

MSIG Europe impulsa su apuesta por el negocio marítimo y de transportes en España

MSIG Europe presentó ayer en Madrid su nueva línea de negocio especializada en transportes y marítimo , una iniciativa con la que «refuerza su posicionamiento en uno de los segmentos estratégicos para la compañía en Europa», afirman. José Luis Sánchez, country manager de MSIG Europe en España , destacó que este lanzamiento “fortalece la capacidad operativa y técnica de la compañía” y refuerza el compromiso de ofrecer soluciones “cada vez más completas y especializadas” para los clientes. Por su parte, Alex Mahnke, CMO de MSIG Europe, y Tom Oostra, CEO de MSIG Specialty Marine , subrayaron que esta nueva etapa permitirá acelerar el crecimiento de la compañía en segmentos estratégicos y responder con mayor agilidad a las necesidades de un mercado en constante transformación.

INESE · ayer · 1 min General

La venta necesita método: del seguimiento informal al control real de oportunidades

Pero la realidad es que cuesta responder a preguntas básicas: qué oportunidades están realmente vivas, cuáles necesitan seguimiento, qué presupuestos están pendientes de respuesta o por qué algunas operaciones se pierden. La actividad comercial en las corredurías es interminable, intensa, amplia, inacabable: llamadas, presupuestos enviados, contactos nuevos, clientes que preguntan, oportunidades que parecen avanzar y otras que se quedan paradas. Por eso, vender no puede consistir solo en generar oportunidades y confiar en que algunas acaben cerrándose.

𝕏 in
INESE · ayer · 1 min General

Multirriesgos: ¿hacia dónde vamos?

En un contexto donde hogares, comercios y empresas son cada vez más tecnológicos y domotizados, la conectividad permitirá a las aseguradoras evolucionar hacia modelos de protección mucho más dinámicos y preventivos . Modelos de cobertura y tarificación que tengan en cuenta si un hogar, comercio o empresa dispone de sensores capaces de detectar una fuga antes de que provoque daños importantes, sistemas que identifiquen anomalías eléctricas, dispositivos conectados que monitoricen consumos o alerten de incidencias o avisos automáticos ante fenómenos meteorológicos extremos son solo algunos ejemplos de cómo el seguro podría evolucionar hacia una protección mucho más activa, inteligente y continua . La historia del seguro siempre ha ido ligada a la evolución de la sociedad.

Artemis · ayer · 1 min 💰 Inversión General

SCOR delighted with cat bond investor support, efficiency gains of reusable structure: Rüede

French global reinsurance company SCOR has highlighted its pleasure with the investor support received for its recently priced $75 million Atlas Capital DAC (Series 2026-1) catastrophe bond and the fact the issuance structure has now been reused for a fourth issuance, delivering efficiency gains. “SCOR is a longstanding sponsor of cat bonds, and we are delighted by the continued investor support, as cat bonds are an integral part of our risk partnerships strategy under the Forward 2026 plan.” Rüede also highlighted the fact the issuance vehicle has now been utilised for a fourth year in a row, saying, “We are also very pleased with the efficiency gains made by reusing Atlas Capital DAC for a fourth year.” SCOR has been sponsoring catastrophe bonds to access the capital for retrocession purposes since the year 2000 and you can read about all of them by filtering our Deal Directory by sponsor . SCOR also highlighted that, “The cat bond offering integrates ESG-related considerations to support investors’ due diligence.” The company said the cat bond was “well received” and “supported by healthy investor demand,” noting the assistance of GC Securities as Sole Structuring Agent and Sole Bookrunner for the deal, while Willkie Farr and Walkers advised SCOR as its legal counsels.

VIG reports Q1’26 profit before taxes of €310.3m as GWP expand 4.4%

Vienna Insurance Group (VIG) has disclosed Q1 2026 profit before taxes of €310.3 million, a significant 18.8% increase over the same period of 2025, driven by the firm’s Special Markets, Poland, Extended CEE and Austria segments. “The very encouraging business development in the first quarter of 2026 means that we can confirm our outlook of achieving profit before taxes in the range of 1.25 to 1.30 billion euro (excluding Nürnberger) for 2026.” Meanwhile, VIG reported that gross written premiums expanded by 4.4% year on year to €4.857 billion in Q1 2026, with the strongest growth recorded in the Czech Republic (+8.4%), followed by Austria (+4.5%), and both Poland and the Extended CEE segment (+5.0% each).

company that contained PFAS, or “forever chemicals,” seeking more than A$2 billion ($1.43 billion) in damages. Read more: EU Restricts Use of ‘Forever Chemicals’ in Firefighting Foams “The Commonwealth is seeking more than A$2 billion in damages to recover significant past and future expenses incurred in investigating and managing contamination resulting from the historical storage and use of this foam.” The government, which used the firefighting foam at 28 defense bases across the country, alleged 3M gave assurances the substance was safe to dispose of, biodegradable and non-toxic. Supreme Court Rejects Florida’s Bid to Sue Western States Over Immigrant CDLs

The Supreme Court on Tuesday rejected Florida’s long shot attempt to sue California and Washington state over the issuance of commercial driver licenses to truckers who don’t speak English and are not authorized to be in the United States. Republican-led Florida has accused the Western states, led by Democrats, of openly defying immigration laws and asked the justices to rule that states lack the authority to issue CDLs to people who are not citizens or legal permanent residents. The Supreme Court typically hears appeals of lower-court decisions, but it sometimes takes on what are known as original lawsuits in which states sue each other in the nation’s highest court.

Carrier Management · ayer · 1 min General

NTSB Calls for FAA Revision of Runway Safety Assessments During Heavy Rainfall

National Transportation Safety Board on Tuesday called on the Federal Aviation Administration to revise how it assesses runway conditions during heavy rainfall, citing safety risks of airplanes skidding off the runway. The NTSB also recommended the FAA issue new rainfall intensity descriptors for aviation weather reports to identify rainfall intensities exceeding the current heavy rain threshold of 0.3 inch (7.62 mm) per hour. The NTSB said wheel braking friction calculations for wet runways can be substantially less than those specified by FAA runway condition assessments due to limitations in the factors considered.

Buckle and subsidiary Gateway sue China Construction Bank over Vesttoo LOC fraud

Buckle, now a fronting specialist for MGA programs and previously a rideshare and gig insurance company, has become the latest to reveal issues caused by fraudulent reinsurance letters of credit linked to Vesttoo and is now suing the purported LOC provider China Construction Bank for damages. The court action arose as Buckle claims China Construction Bank (CCB) failed to honour the LOCs, that were supposed to have collateralized reinsurance obligations owed to Gateway. Nine letters of credit are the subject of Buckle’s lawsuit in an Illinois court against China Construction Bank entities, with the plaintiff claiming the bank had signed off on $9,799,895 of collateral across the nine LOCs.

𝕏 in
Insurance Journal · ayer · 1 min General

India’s Prized Alphonso Mango Crop Ruined by Weather

Maharashtra is renowned for its Alphonso mangoes, but officials say hotter weather has ruined this year’s crop of the variety known as the “King of Mangoes.” A sharp difference in day and nighttime temperatures in December and January hurt flowering and fruit setting, while hotter than usual weather in April and May, probably due to the El Niño weather phenomenon, then spoiled the fruits themselves, said Bapusaheb Manikrao Lambade, a government agriculture officer in Devgad, one of Maharashtra’s top Alphonso-growing areas. In India’s western state of Maharashtra, mango farmer Komal Walke is scrambling to meet orders from India’s online grocers after her family’s three acres of orchards produced almost no Alphonso mangoes this year. India’s entire mango crop was worth $2.3 billion last year, according to Indian research firm Mordor Intelligence, which expects the market to grow to $3.4 billion by 2031.

𝕏 in
Insurance Journal · ayer · 1 min ⚖️ Regulación General

Northwestern Medicine Agrees to Pay $325K in Religious Discrimination Case

Northwestern Medicine agreed to pay $325,000 in monetary relief to a class of employees denied vaccine exemptions because of their religious beliefs or practices and to provide other relief following an investigation by the U.S. The agreement resolves charges filed with the EEOC alleging discrimination against employees based on their religion when it denied reasonable accommodation requests to employees who requested a faith-based exemption to Northwestern Medicine’s mandatory flu vaccination policy. The EEOC’s investigation found that Northwestern Medicine discriminated against a class of employees from November 10, 2023, to the present at its facilities across Illinois by denying these employees a religious accommodation and by denying them the opportunity to earn an annual bonus which the employer described as a “vaccine incentive bonus,” intended to motivate compliance with the vaccination mandate policy.

💡Puede requerir adaptación regulatoria en los próximos meses.

4 Arrested in Louisiana Insurance Fraud Scheme

The Louisiana State Police Insurance Fraud / Auto Theft Unit (IFAT) has arrested four individuals following an investigation into an alleged automobile insurance fraud scheme in Iberia Parish The investigation began after a criminal referral from Timber Creek Insurance Services and the Louisiana Department of Insurance Fraud Division regarding allegations of fraudulent insurance claims involving 29-year-old Rayshonte Oppenheimer, 32-year-old Briannie Butler, 24-year-old Passion Keal, and 33-year-old Quandalyn Bernard, all of New Iberia. Between April 30 and May 1, 2026, investigators obtained arrest warrants for all four suspects for Automobile Insurance Fraud (Felony). The investigation determined the suspects made false statements during the insurance claims process following a 2025 crash involving a Pelican Waste & Debris garbage truck.

Founded last year, Pace develops AI agents designed specifically for insurance workflows spanning submissions, renewals, policy servicing and claims operations. Storm Counts, Landfalls and Losses: The Hidden Risk Behind a ‘Quiet’ Hurricane Season

Some observers may label the 2025 Atlantic hurricane season as a “quiet” year due to zero U.S. hurricane landfalls, but from an insurance and catastrophe risk perspective, that conclusion misses the point—and, potentially, the risk.

We’re inviting applications from senior practitioners at smaller regulated firms in the general insurance and consumer credit sectors to join the panel. Review of financial promotion approvers finds some firms need to raise standards

Firms that approve financial promotions should be doing more to protect consumers, an FCA review has found. 'Firms must make sure every promotion they sign off is fair, clear and not misleading.' As a result of the FCA's work, one firm has already had to conduct a remediation exercise and some websites have been blocked to retail customers. Korean Re secures targeted $75m multi-peril retro through second Solomon Re cat bond

Korean Re, the global reinsurance company headquartered in the country of its name, has now secured the targeted $75 million of multi-peril retrocession from its Solomon Re Ltd. Nearly three years ago, Korean Re sponsored its debut catastrophe bond, securing $75 million in US multi-peril catastrophe retrocession from its first Solomon Re 2023-1 issuance. As we previously explained, while the first Solomon Re cat bond only covered US named storm and earthquake risks for Korean Re, through this Solomon Re Series 2026-1 issuance, Korean Re will receive a source of retrocession for Israel earthquake risks, which is a peril rarely seen in the market.

Mercury targets $100m California fire reinsurance with second Luca Re catastrophe bond

Mercury General Corporation (Mercury Insurance), the California headquartered insurer, is back in the catastrophe bond market seeking additional fire reinsurance protection, with an initial $100 million target for its second 144A cat bond sponsorship of a Luca Re Ltd. Mercury Insurance sponsored its first 144A catastrophe bond last year, securing $150 million in California fire reinsurance protection from the capital markets with its debut Luca Re 2025-1 deal. Having secured $150 million of California wildfire and fire following earthquake protection from the capital markets with its first Luca Re cat bond a year ago, we’ve learned that Mercury is now back and looking to extend that reinsurance with its second 144A cat bond sponsorship.

💡Impacto directo en exposición, pricing y reservas del sector.

Artemis · ayer · 1 min 💰 Inversión General

Oak raises target size of Quercian Re 2026-1 cat bond, now seeks up to $150m

Oak Global, the specialist Lloyd’s underwriting company, has now raised the target size for its debut catastrophe bond sponsorship, with retrocessional protection from the Arthur Re Ltd. – Quercian Re 2026-1 cat bond issuance now expected to fall between $125 million and as much as $150 million, Artemis understands. Oak Global made its debut in the catastrophe bond market earlier this month, with this Quercian Re 2026-1 issuance set to become the first catastrophe bond that will benefit Oak Reinsurance Syndicate 2843, a syndicate focused on reinsurance opportunities that is operated by specialist underwriter in the Lloyd’s market Oak Global.

Canada Life agrees £36m pension buy-in with HSB Engineering Insurance Services scheme

Canada Life, a UK insurer providing life insurance and pension risk transfer services, has completed a £36 million full-scheme buy-in with the pension scheme of HSB Engineering Insurance Services Limited. The sponsoring employer, HSB UK & Ireland, part of a UK underwriting business specialising in engineering and technology insurance and inspection services across construction, manufacturing, leisure, retail and commercial sectors as well as public services, supported the arrangement alongside Canada Life. The buy-in forms part of ongoing pension risk transfer activity in the UK, with Canada Life working with the scheme and its advisers to complete the agreement in a coordinated process involving all parties.

I am delighted to announce the appointment of Monique for General Manager across Netherlands, Belgium and the Nordics – all regions we are investing in as we execute our Invest in Europe strategy and drive towards our 2030 ambitions.” Liberty Specialty Markets noted that, while at CNA Hardy, Kooijman served as Regional Director with oversight of the Benelux countries, Germany and the Nordics.

Insurance Journal · ayer · 1 min General

OpenAI’s Altman Says AI Unlikely to Lead to ‘Jobs Apocalypse’

OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said on Tuesday the rapid development and adoption of AI would not lead to a global “jobs apocalypse” and the technology had not claimed as many white-collar jobs as he had feared. “I’m delighted to be wrong about this, I thought there would have been more impact on entry-level white-collar jobs being eliminated by now than has actually happened,” Altman told CBA Chief Executive Matt Comyn in an interview. “People are like ‘oh you could have saved the world a lot of fear mongering and a lot of doom and gloom’ but at the time I was like ‘I see this is a real risk we should probably talk about it’ and it still may.” Altman did not cite any jobs numbers on Tuesday but has previously talked about potential industry-wide job cuts due to AI’s advancement.

Insurance Journal · ayer · 1 min General

California Officials Lift Evacuation Orders for Residents Near Damaged Chemical Tank

Southern California officials lifted the final evacuation orders Tuesday night for residents who live near a damaged chemical tank, allowing 16,000 people to return home. “All residents will go home,” Orange County Fire Authority Division Chief Craig Covey said during a meeting that grew raucous at times as residents questioned why the chemical was allowed in a densely populated area and urged city officials to hold the company that operates the tank accountable. Authorities announced they were lifting the final orders after the temperature on the tank remained stable for four hours without intervention from sprinklers.

An accident at a Washington state packaging facility has left at least one person dead, nine missing and nine others injured, with crews working to contain a ruptured tank of caustic chemicals. The incident occurred Tuesday morning at Nippon Dynawave Packaging Co.’s facility in Longview, as a massive industrial tank of “white liquor” collapsed, officials said. Officials did not say what caused the explosion, Charlotte-based WBTV reported.

onLygal ha anunciado el nombramiento de Rubén Gómez Gismera como delegado territorial para la zona Centro, asumiendo también la responsabilidad de alianzas estratégicas en el Canal Mediado . En este sentido, reportará a Ramón Gómez, director comercial del Canal Mediado. Licenciado en Economía y en Ciencias Actuariales y Financieras por la Universidad Carlos III de Madrid, con postgrado en Ahorro y Previsión por la UPF Barcelona School of Management y formación como corredor de seguros, aporta más de 20 años de experiencia en el sector asegurador , con una trayectoria tanto en gestión técnica como en dirección comercial y desarrollo de alianzas con aseguradoras y empresas tecnológicas.

Como anunció ayer a través de su perfil de LinkedIn Gianluca Piscopo, managing director, la oficina de Munich Re Specialty Madrid ha reforzado su equipo de Reclamaciones con Guillermo Hoces Hurtado como country claims manager. Procede de Everest , donde llevaba algo más de dos años y donde hasta ahora ocupaba el cargo de deputy head of claims Continental Europe. Leer artículo →
𝕏 in
INESE · hace 2 días · 1 min 🤝 M&A General

Allianz y WWF invertirán 270.000 euros en restaurar el río Mijares tras la DANA

Allianz y WWF han puesto en marcha un proyecto de restauración ecológica en el entorno del Río Mijares, entre los municipios valencianos de Buñol y Yátova, con el objetivo de recuperar los ecosistemas afectados por la DANA de octubre de 2024 y reforzar la resiliencia del territorio frente a futuros episodios climáticos extremos. La iniciativa contará con una inversión de 270.000 euros durante los próximos tres años y contempla actuaciones sobre el cauce y la vegetación de ribera para mejorar la capacidad natural del río de absorber crecidas y reducir el impacto de posibles inundaciones. Entre las medidas previstas figuran la eliminación de especies invasoras, la limpieza selectiva del cauce, la instalación de estructuras naturales para reducir la fuerza del agua y la reforestación con especies autóctonas.

💡Consolida el mercado y puede redefinir la competencia en el sector.

Workers’ compensation is entering a period of heightened complexity as regulatory changes and policy shifts continue to unfold across federal and state levels. Although workers’ comp is governed at the state level, federal developments continue to influence the broader property/casualty landscape in meaningful ways. So far this year, a surge in legislative activity, combined with evolving federal healthcare policy and persistent medical cost pressures, is reshaping the operating environment.

Artemis · hace 2 días · 1 min 💰 Inversión General

Allstate returns some Sanders 2022-1 aggregate cat bond principal to investors, extends rest

Allstate has returned some principal from one of its outstanding aggregate catastrophe bonds to investors, while extending the maturity on the remaining amount, having ended the last annual aggregate risk period that its cat bonds were subject to with total pre-tax reported catastrophe losses just shy of $4 billion. We don’t know what the qualifying loss accumulation was against these Sanders Re III 2022-1 Class C notes, but we’ve now learned that at the end of the annual risk period some of their principal was returned to investors, with the remainder now having an extended maturity date. Recall that Allstate’s annual aggregate risk period for its nationwide (ex-Florida) catastrophe bond backed reinsurance protection runs from April 1st each year.

Artemis · hace 2 días · 1 min General

The return of “Humpty Dumpty” as Florida reinsurance conditions ease?

As reinsurance market conditions ease meaningfully for Florida based insurers at the mid-year renewals, an analyst report has cited American Integrity CEO Bob Ritchie as saying the easing is around more than just price, with improvements in terms and conditions suggesting “Humpty Dumpty is getting put back together.” TD Cowen analysts met with a number of insurance market participants in the Tampa Bay area recently, concluding that the Florida market is loosening at primary and reinsurance levels, while hearing of irrational behaviour from some newer market participants. Reinsurance market dynamics have always been a driver for competitive tendencies in Florida’s insurance market and with more pronounced price softening being seen at these reinsurance renewals, with rates said down 15% +, it’s leading to weaker terms and conditions, the analysts said. American Integrity Insurance CEO Bob Ritchie told the analysts that “Humpty Dumpty is getting put back together,” as reinsurance coverage options improve for protection buyers.

Addresscloud adds Skyblu’s high-resolution European wildfire model

Addresscloud, a UK geocoding and property intelligence expert, has adopted the high-resolution, pan-European wildfire risk model developed by Skyblu, an AI solutions provider. Skyblu’s high-resolution new model delivers 20-metre resolution risk scoring across Europe, offering significantly greater granularity than traditional datasets. The pair said that this partnership reflects growing demand for high-resolution climate intelligence in underwriting, with both companies already supporting wind and hail risk modelling in selected markets such as France and Spain, and planning a broader European rollout.

Weak cyber hygiene among smaller businesses exposes protection gap: KYND

Smaller businesses exhibit widespread weaknesses in cyber hygiene, including a lack of basic email security protections, highlighting cyber exposure and a significant opportunity for insurers to support improved resilience, according to Ben Duffy, VP and Head of North America at KYND. Cyber risk intelligence provider KYND has released research based on 7,980 small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs) across the US and Canada, and 830 UK small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs). Despite this, cyber insurance penetration among SMEs and SMBs remains relatively low — often estimated at below 10% in many segments — highlighting a substantial gap between cyber exposure and protection.

Bringing them together is a defining moment for both the market and SiriusPoint, giving us the scale, expertise and credibility from day one to build a disciplined, durable portfolio.” David Govrin, Group President and Chief Executive Officer, Global Reinsurance & London Market Specialty, SiriusPoint, said, “The launch of Crisis Solutions and the hiring of Ed and Paul shortly after the establishment of our London Market Specialty division reflect our ambition to grow our position in the specialty classes where global clients have always sought the innovation, product expertise, and capacity of the London Market. The team will be based in London, with plans to build out underwriting, exposure management, and claims capabilities, supporting the Crisis Solutions business and SiriusPoint’s next phase of growth within the London Market Specialty division.

New York-based insurance broker USI Insurance Services has gone to court to halt a former employee it alleges has poached a few customers for his own newly-formed agency. According to USI, MacNair has used “USI’s confidential information and its reputation in the insurance industry to build the book of business he presided over on behalf of USI” and is now using that information and those relationships to build his new agency, USI is seeking monetary damages and an injunction to stop MacNair’s alleged “ongoing violations” of his agreements with USI. MacNair is violating employment and severance agreements by using confidential information and soliciting his former clients for his own Pennsylvania-based MacNair Enterprises LLC in direct competition with USI.

Related: California Revokes 17,000 Commercial Driver’s Licenses for Immigrants North Carolina May Lose $50M in Federal Funds Over Immigrant Trucker Licenses Giving no explanation, the justices on Tuesday rejected Florida’s bid to sue its fellow states directly in the Supreme Court, as states can do under some circumstances to protect their sovereign interests.

𝕏 in
Insurance Journal · hace 2 días · 1 min General

Viewpoint: Tackling the Threat of Legal System Abuse

The data strongly suggest the answer is legal system abuse – exploitative attorney advertising, litigation financing, and plaintiff attorney tactics that are generating litigation independently of underlying accident trends. Research conducted by the Insurance Information Institute and the Casualty Actuarial Society found that legal system abuse and related litigation trends have resulted in an estimated $231.6 billion to $281.2 billion increase in liability insurance losses over the past decade. Third-party litigation financing (TPLF) is when a funder unrelated to the case, such as a hedge fund, provides money to cover a plaintiff’s legal costs in exchange for a share of any damages awarded through judgment or settlement.

Insurance Innovation Reporter · hace 2 días · 1 min 🌪️ Clima & Catástrofes General

Addresscloud Adds European Wildfire Model

Addresscloud (Cambridge, England), a geocoding and property intelligence provider, has added a high-resolution pan-European wildfire risk model developed by Skyblu (Warsaw) to its platform. The move expands Addresscloud’s portfolio of wildfire risk data for insurers operating in Europe, with more than double the area burned in recent years compared with the early 2000s putting pressure on insurers to improve risk selection and pricing, according to the companies. Built using satellite observation data from 2001 to 2023 and enriched with climate and vegetation data, the model provides forward-looking wildfire probability insights for 2026 to 2030.

and Iran still had to agree on what to do about Iran's stockpile of enriched uranium, about reopening the Strait of Hormuz and about U.S.-led economic sanctions against Iran. and Iran was 95% complete, I was reminded of a truism among software developers in the 1990s: "The first 80% of a project takes 90% of the time, and the final 20% takes 90% of the time." Del core a los componentes: la banca acelera una transformación sin “big bang”, pero con negocios más enfocados

La banca latinoamericana avanza hacia un modelo de modernización progresiva basado en componentes, dejando atrás las grandes implementaciones y migraciones “big bang” para adoptar plataformas más flexibles, abiertas y conectadas con los ecosistemas financieros, sin interrumpir la continuidad del negocio. Regístrate gratis o inicia sesión para leer el artículo completo y descubrir: Perspectivas estratégicas sobre innovación, regulación y tendencias en banca digital y fintech Entrevistas con líderes que están transformando el sector financiero en la región Reportes y estudios que anticipan los cambios clave del mercado Acceso prioritario a información para tomar decisiones informadas y fortalecer tu liderazgo Únete a la comunidad de ejecutivos que marcan la diferencia en la transformación digital de la banca. Durante años, los proyectos de transformación bancaria estuvieron marcados por un modelo que obligaba a esperar meses, incluso años, hasta tener todo listo antes de ejecutar el cambio.

KatRisk Joins Verisk Model Exchange

Verisk (Jersey City, N.J.) has announced that KatRisk has entered into an agreement to join Verisk Model Exchange, expanding access to third-party catastrophe risk models within Verisk’s open catastrophe risk modeling ecosystem. Adding KatRisk models to Verisk Model Exchange is intended to expand access to specialized catastrophe risk insights, particularly for new geographic territories and perils where additional modeling perspectives can support clearer understanding of exposure and resilience. “Verisk Model Exchange will allow the market to assess KatRisk’s models alongside other independent approaches within a common analytical framework supporting informed comparison and stronger risk insight across the industry.” Models available through Verisk Model Exchange span global natural catastrophe perils, including wildfire, severe convective storm, tropical cyclone, storm surge, inland flood and earthquake.

Milder than Normal Atlantic Hurricane Season Expected Thanks to El Niño

A developing El Niño that is forecast to get quite strong will likely dampen the upcoming Atlantic hurricane season, but it won’t make the potentially deadly storms disappear, federal and outside meteorologists predict. A normal hurricane season has 14 named storms, seven of them becoming hurricanes and three of them reaching major hurricane level, which is more than 110 mph (177 kph). This is after nine of the last 10 Atlantic hurricane seasons have been above normal or even hyperactive, Klotzbach said.

ILS valuation now a ‘core determinant’ of market credibility, Smyth, Nascent Group

In recent years, the insurance-linked securities (ILS) market has expanded from a niche property catastrophe tool into a broader and more complex asset class, and with that evolution, valuation has also moved from a reporting exercise into a core determinant of market credibility, according to Andy Smyth of Nascent Group. With that evolution, valuation has moved from a reporting exercise to a core determinant of market credibility,” Smyth explains in a recent report. To conclude, Smyth observes that valuation encompasses both governance and technical aspects, highlighting how investors anticipate independence, transparency, and a clear expression of uncertainty, all of which are vital for maintaining market credibility.

Verisk said the move is aimed at increasing access to third-party catastrophe risk insights for insurers and reinsurers, as climate-related hazards and changing regulatory requirements continue to raise expectations for clear and supportable risk assessment.

𝕏 in
Insurance Journal · hace 2 días · 1 min General

JPMorgan Banker Sues Ex-Colleague Over ‘Fabricated’ Sex Claims

banker named in a graphic sexual harassment suit that went viral last month hit back at her accuser with a defamation counterclaim, calling his allegations “entirely false” and “malicious.” Lorna Hajdini last Tuesday night countersued a former colleague on the bank’s leveraged finance team who claimed that she forced him to be her “sex slave” and used racist language to demean him. She “categorically and unequivocally” denied his “disgusting” claims, saying they have “ruined her reputation and destroyed her life.” The April 27 suit against JPMorgan and Hajdini was filed by a John Doe plaintiff, but people familiar with the matter have said he was Chirayu Rana, a former vice president at the bank. Hajdini and members of her family have been mocked, ridiculed, and harassed around the clock,” lawyers for the banker said, “with Ms.

Artemis · hace 3 días · 1 min 🌪️ Clima & Catástrofes General

56% of planned US data centres are in catastrophe exposed locations: MS Amlin

A new analysis from Lloyd’s global specialist re/insurer MS Amlin has revealed that 56% of the 670 planned US data centre projects representing nearly $800 billion in investment, are located in states that are highly exposed to either hurricanes, severe convective storms, earthquakes or winterstorms. In addition, MS Amlin also noted that 27% of data centres, representing $440 billion in investment, are planned in states at high risk of winter storms, which can disrupt power networks and create complex business interruption risks. MS Amlin also observed that nearly a quarter (21%) of planned data centres, amounting to $340 billion of investment, are located in states at high risk of hurricanes, while data centres in high-risk earthquake states account for 3% of planned facilities, representing roughly $12 billion in investment.

Galapagos Capital SSPE issues largest LRS at R$126m, the fourth under Brazil’s ILS regime

Gálapagos Capital Sociedade Seguradora de Propósito Específico S.A., the Brazil based SSPE or special purpose reinsurance vehicle that can issue Letra de Risco de Seguro (Insurance Risk Letters) and is owned by global investment company Galapagos Capital, has completed issuance of the largest transaction under Brazil’s insurance-linked securities regime so far. Gálapagos Capital SSPE has issued a R$126 million offering of LRS which is the first broadly distributed LRS arrangement since Brazil put its own ILS regulations into law and activity commenced in the country. The model represents an important shift in the traditional market logic by establishing a direct link between those who take risks and those seeking investment opportunities.” Roberto Takatsu, partner in the insurance business at Galapagos Capital, highlighted how this larger and more broadly distributed offering demonstrates Brazil’s ILS market activity gaining pace, “The accelerated dissemination of this instrument in the market implies a long learning period, which is only now gaining more traction.

California Narrows Evacuation Area With Risk ‘Off the Table’

There is no longer a risk of a compromised chemical tank exploding in the suburbs of Los Angeles and evacuation orders for some of the more than 50,000 people were lifted, according to the Orange County Fire Authority. On Monday, the Orange County Fire Authority interim chief said the threat of explosion was now “off the table” after firefighters found and verified a crack that relieved pressure in the tank. As the crisis stretches on, schools in the affected area have rescheduled graduations and told parents to prepare for online learning on Tuesday.

US Supreme Court Denies Meta’s Challenge to Vermont Social Media Addiction Lawsuit

Supreme Court declined on Tuesday to hear a bid by Meta Platforms to avoid a lawsuit brought by Vermont’s attorney general accusing the company of designing its Instagram social media app to be addictive to young users, as big technology companies face mounting legal risks over child and teen safety. The justices turned away Meta’s appeal of a lower court’s ruling that let the lawsuit proceed, rejecting the company’s argument that courts in Vermont lack jurisdiction over the dispute. Testifying in February at a youth social media addiction trial in California, Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg denied that Instagram targets kids.

La agencia recomienda a las aseguradoras integrar los brotes de enfermedades en la gestión de riesgos corporativos y realizar pruebas de estrés periódicas sobre riesgos no modelizados. Ashley Alder, chair of the FCA, said: 'Bringing our Annual Public Meeting to Edinburgh for the first time is a deliberate step to strengthen our visibility and accountability right across the UK. LGT reinsurer Lumen Re maintains its lower loss ratio, premiums written increasing

Lumen Re, the main Bermuda-domiciled rated reinsurance underwriting vehicle of specialist insurance-linked securities manager LGT ILS Partners, continued to exhibit a lower loss ratio last year according to AM Best, while its latest financial report shows the gross premiums underwritten rose 23% in 2024 to approaching $207 million. While accident year losses were a little higher in 2024 than 2023, AM Best noted in its latest assessment that improvement has continued over time, saying that Lumen Re’s “loss ratio has decreased significantly since 2022.” Lumen Re continues to underwrite natural catastrophe exposed reinsurance, channelling that to entities in the LGT ILS Partners business structure, to efficiently originate opportunities for certain of the managers ILS funds. rating affirmation by AM Best, the agency explained that the Class 3B reinsurer has again renewed its status as a reciprocal jurisdiction reinsurance company in 37 US states for 2026 It’s now the third year in a row the LGT ILS Partners linked reinsurer has kept this status intact, which AM Best noted “reduces operational burden and costs” for Lumen Re.

US Coastal insurers secure $100m Chartwell Re cat bond at reduced pricing

As we later reported , the target size of the issuance remained the same but the sponsoring insurers, US Coastal Insurance Company and US Coastal Property & Casualty Insurance Company, were aiming to secure the reinsurance protection at reduced pricing, capitalising on strong execution in the cat bond market. The US Coastal insurers returned to the cat bond market earlier this month, looking to expand the role catastrophe bonds play in their reinsurance tower, with an initial target to secure $100 million of fully-collateralized reinsurance from this new Chartwell Re Series 2026-1 offering. So the Chartwell Re Series 2026-1 cat bond notes are now confirmed to provide $100 million of catastrophe reinsurance to the two US Coastal insurers on an indemnity trigger and per-occurrence basis, across a three year term and covering named storm losses in the states of Alabama, Florida, Mississippi, New Jersey, New York, Rhode Island, and Texas.

Artemis · hace 3 días · 1 min 💰 Inversión 💰 $1500M General

More details on State Farm’s new $1.5bn Merna Re cat bonds, all annual aggregate protection

These Merna Re Enterprise II 2026-1 cat bond notes will provide State Farm with a $1.5 billion roughly three year source of collateralized reinsurance protection, running to maturity in early July 2029 When we learned about this deal we did not know the exact perils covered, although we assumed they would likely cover multiple perils on an indemnity basis as is typical of State Farm’s recent cat bonds, or whether the $1.5 billion of coverage was per-occurrence or annual aggregate. Now we have learned that both tranches of notes will provide State Farm with the roughly three years of reinsurance protection on an annual aggregate and indemnity trigger basis. We’ve now updated our previous article to reflect the correct name for the SPI that issued the new cat bonds for State Farm and amended our Deal Directory entry for the issuance to be Merna Re Enterprise II Ltd.

Insurers better prepared to deal with disease outbreaks since COVID: AM Best

The latest outbreaks are putting a renewed focus on the insurance implications of the spread of rare communicable diseases and the need for proactive risk management, according to AM Best’s commentary, “Insurers Better Prepared For Rare Disease Outbreak Amid Lessons From COVID-19.” Health experts have said that currently, neither virus poses an immediate global threat nor is expected to spread anywhere near the scale of COVID-19. Credit ratings agency AM Best finds that insurance companies are well-prepared to handle any direct impact from the recent outbreaks of the Ebola virus and Andes hantavirus, thanks in part to the “lessons learned” from the COVID-19 pandemic in coverage gaps and systemic vulnerabilities. Saving Supply Chains From Climate Shocks Is a Lure for Investors

The intervention earlier this month is an example of how nascent technology can help protect the world’s $35 trillion global trade system , not just from geopolitical disruption and human error, but also from an increasingly unpredictable climate. Those threats are delivering new demand for adaptation strategies — everything from sensor manufacturers and climate risk analytics tools to insurance based on catastrophe modeling — and fueling a small but growing investment class offering unexpected returns. Advances in satellite imagery, more granular weather data, 5G networks and artificial intelligence are also helping to propel new climate tech start-ups.

War Overtakes Civil Unrest as Top Political Violence Exposure for Global Businesses

War has overtaken civil unrest as the political violence exposure that companies fear most, as conflicts in Europe and the Middle East disrupt global trade flows, strain political alliances, embolden adversarial powers and heighten risks to business assets, according to the Allianz Risk Barometer 2026 . War was identified as the political violence exposure most feared by companies, surpassing civil unrest to garner 53% of the votes in the 2026 Allianz Risk Barometer, climbing from 48% in 2025. Around 60% of respondents from Europe and Asia-Pacific see war as the top political risk and violence exposure, while civil unrest ranks No.

FBI Prepares for Increasing Drone Threats Disrupting World Cup

The FBI plans to deploy roughly 60 specially trained state and local police officers across venues for the FIFA World Cup to detect and electronically disable hostile drones as security officials warn that threats once associated with foreign battlefields are increasingly possible inside the US. “Every major public gathering, from a World Cup match to an America 250 celebration, is now a drone environment,” Devin Kowalski, an FBI assistant director who oversees the Critical Incident Response Group, said during the exercise. Officers from agencies including state police in New York and New Jersey, the New York City and Los Angeles police departments and the Miami-Dade Sheriff’s Office will operate counter-drone equipment under FBI supervision, according to a person familiar with the program who isn’t authorized to speak publicly.

INESE · hace 4 días · 1 min General

«Los resultados importan, pero el liderazgo se construye en el cómo»

“El mundo empresarial ha evolucionado desde una orientación casi exclusiva a los resultados hacia un modelo en el que sigue importando el qué, pero cada vez tiene más peso el cómo”, explicó. El liderazgo ya no se mide solo por los resultados, ahora es igual de importante la cultura, los comportamientos y la forma de trabajar, afirma Roberto Muñoz, director general de Santander Seguros, quien compartió con las alumnas del curso de liderazgo femenino Next Generation Women in Insurance, impulsado por #RedEWI, su experiencia profesional y su visión sobre la evolución del liderazgo, la gestión de personas y la transformación de las organizaciones. A lo largo de la sesión, destacó cómo ha cambiado el concepto de liderazgo en las empresas y la creciente importancia de la cultura corporativa y de las personas.

El costo-beneficio detrás de la continuidad operativa, la estrategia de Spin

Los respaldos, redundancia en la nube y los planes de continuidad ya no se justifican solo como inversiones técnicas: hoy necesitan demostrar su valor frente al negocio con una evaluación de costo–beneficio donde los indicadores de seguridad demuestren su impacto en la operación. La tendencia reciente es que, más que preguntar cuánto se gastó en seguridad, los directorios y áreas de negocio exigen demostrar qué riesgos se reducen y qué servicios siguen operando. 𝕏 in
INESE · hace 4 días · 1 min 💰 Inversión General

El beneficio de las aseguradoras sube un 5% en los tres primeros meses de 2026

Las estadísticas de Icea muestran un ligero aumento de la siniestralidad media del conjunto de aseguradoras desde el 74,35% de las primas imputadas de negocio directo en marzo de 2025 al 75,34% al cierre de los tres primeros meses de este año. Con un resultado de la cuenta no técnica del 8,04% de las primas imputadas de negocio retenido en el primer trimestre del año (8,34% en el mismo periodo de 2025), las aseguradoras alcanzaron en el inicio del ejercicio un beneficio neto conjunto de 1.696 millones de euros, un 4,7% más que doce meses antes. En líneas generales, las aseguradoras inician el año con una ralentización en el incremento de sus resultados respecto a 2025, un ejercicio que se cerró con una mejora del 15% en las ganancias.

¿Son caros los seguros en España? Un tercio de los consumidores cree que sí

La presión del coste de vida impacta con mayor intensidad en los consumidores españoles que en el resto de mercados europeos analizados en el ‘Estudio Europeo de Consumidores de Seguros 2026′, de Guidewire. En España y Francia, el 26% y el 32%, respectivamente, consideran que los productos del sector son caros. En otra de las cuestiones analizadas, España es el país con mayor proporción de consumidores favorables a que las aseguradoras recopilen información a través de sensores y dispositivos conectados : un 40% valora esta práctica de forma positiva, frente al 36% que la rechaza.

Banorte Extends Risk Management with AI Signals

IIR met Abraham Izquierdo, Managing Director of Treasury Risk, Banorte, at SAS Innovate 2026 in Grapevine, Texas, where SAS introduced him as a customer whose work in banking risk management had relevance beyond banking. Izquierdo described how Banorte is using SAS technology, core banking data and in-house machine learning to connect liquidity-risk monitoring with external sentiment signals. “One of those is risk management.” From Balance Sheet Modeling to Broader Risk Analytics Banorte’s AI-enabled liquidity-risk initiative builds on a broader risk analytics program that has included balance sheet modeling and interest-rate risk management, areas where the institution has used SAS technology to model banking-book positions and analyze the behavior of assets, liabilities and hedges.

Calling Cops Delay Doesn’t Wreck UM Claim, West Virginia High Court Rules

But that’s not enough to negate a claim, the West Virginia Supreme Court of Appeals decided this week. “… We conclude that, under the clear and unambiguous language of the subject policy, West Virginia National was required to prove that its investigative interests were prejudiced by petitioners’ failure to report the accident to a police, peace, or judicial officer within twenty-four hours,” the high court wrote in its May 21 opinion . The couple did report the crash to the insurance company within four days, and West Virginia National began its own investigation of the claim.

Airbus, Air France Found Guilty in 2009 Rio-Paris Crash

Airbus SE and Air France were found guilty of involuntary manslaughter in the deadly crash of a passenger jet en route to Paris from Rio de Janeiro 17 years ago after a French appeals court overturned a previous ruling. Top photo: Investigators inspect debris from the mid-Atlantic crash of Air France flight 447 at the CEAT aeronautical laboratory in Toulouse, France in 2009. The companies were solely culpable for the accident that killed all 228 passengers and crew on board the Airbus A330, the Paris appeals court said on Thursday.

Years Before UPS Plane Crash, 10 Similar Flaws Didn’t Raise Alarm

There has been only one other crash involving a similar plane model when the engine fell off decades earlier. After the engine flew off a UPS cargo plane and caused a crash that killed 15 people in Kentucky, investigators quickly discovered cracks in parts of the engine mount. Then they found records showing similar flaws had been found during maintenance on the wings of 10 other planes — most of which had not been reported to the FAA.

Waymo Suspends Freeway Rides Across U.S., Pauses Atlanta Operations

Alphabet’s Waymo said on Thursday it has suspended its robotaxi service on freeways in the United States and paused its operations in Atlanta, Georgia, as it updates software to improve performance around construction zones and flooded roadways. “We have temporarily paused freeway operations, as we work to integrate recent technical learnings into our software and expect to resume these routes soon,” a Waymo spokesperson said in an email. Freeway trips on Waymo were available in San Francisco, Los Angeles, Phoenix, and Miami.

Tradecraft ILS Advisors consultancy launched by former AXIS ILS exec Kyle Freeman

Tradecraft ILS Advisors has been launched as a new independent consulting and advisory firm focused on the insurance-linked securities (ILS) market by well-known industry executive and former AXIS ILS leader Kyle Freeman. With Tradecraft ILS Advisors, Freeman aims to work with organisations to help them in their strategic decision-making, transaction negotiations, contract structuring, and analysis, of insurance-linked securities opportunities and risk transfer arrangements. Under Tradecraft ILS Advisors, Freeman sees an opportunity to bring his experience in a broad range of ILS structures for multiple classes of business and perils, as well as his understanding of investor needs, to clients looking to enter, expand, or innovate in the market.

Catastrophe bond issuance in H1 2026 now projected at $16.3bn, could rise further

Catastrophe bond issuance for the first-half of 2026 is now projected to reach $16.3 billion according to the latest Artemis Deal Directory data, which could rise further as there remain a number of offerings in the market which could upsize, while further new deals could emerge. Catastrophe bond issuance in 2026 has continued to accelerate and the addition of State Farm’s new $1.5 billion of Merna Re Enterprise cat bonds yesterday has given issuance a meaningful boost. Within the 144A cat bond bucket, over $13.72 billion of issuance settled so far in 2026 are property catastrophe bonds, while only $384.5 million cover specialty or life/health related business .

Delay in Calling Police Does Not Wreck UM Claim, WV Supreme Court Says

But that’s not enough to negate a claim, the West Virginia Supreme Court of Appeals decided this week. “… We conclude that, under the clear and unambiguous language of the subject policy, West Virginia National was required to prove that its investigative interests were prejudiced by petitioners’ failure to report the accident to a police, peace, or judicial officer within twenty-four hours,” the high court wrote in its May 21 opinion .
